claim.observation: The Windows Recycle Bin $I metadata file $I6SODDB (NTFS inode 91913) records the original path of the deleted item as C:\Windows\System32\dllhost.
claim.interpretation: The deleted folder $R6SODDB - which contains the implant svchost.exe (F-...-007) and its C2 config winclient.reg (F-...-006) - originally resided at C:\Windows\System32\dllhost. A folder named 'dllhost' placed directly in System32 masquerades as Windows component naming, and System32 is a privileged location requiring administrative rights to write. The whole malware folder was subsequently deleted into the Recycle Bin of the SID ending -1673. This identifies the implant's staging directory and shows an attempt to remove it (anti-forensics), with the contents nonetheless recoverable.
